# R4 · Time, Delay & Priority — Redis Patterns Applied

> The sorted set as a clock: one ZSET read two ways — a timer wheel (score = a future fire-time) and a ranking
> (score = a value to order by). Score a job by its fire-time and a range query sweeps the due head onto the pending
> set; rank players by points and a reverse range reads the leaderboard. Grounded in the real Elixir bus
> echo/apps/echo_mq (EchoMQ.{Jobs,Repeat,Backoff,Lanes,Metronome}) and the codemojex consumer (echo/apps/codemojex).
> Depends on R3's reliable job. Served at /redis-patterns/time-delay-priority. Fully built: the landing, three
> orientation dives, and all six granular modules R4.01–R4.06 (the workshop is the capstone). The patterns are
> properties of the sorted set; the engine is Valkey.

## The chapter — three orientation dives (built)

- [R4 · The sorted set as a clock](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/the-sorted-set-as-a-clock): one data structure, two readings — a timer wheel (emq:{queue}:schedule, score = the run-at millisecond, swept by EchoMQ.Jobs.promote/3) and a ranking (a ZSET scored by a value, read by ZREVRANGE). The member is constant; the score is the meaning.
- [R4 · Score as meaning](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/score-as-meaning): the score is the whole semantic axis — fire-time (now + delay, read off the server TIME), order (the branded JOB id's mint position on the score-0 pending set — the order theorem), and next-run (the EchoMQ.Repeat registry). Same commands (ZADD/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZREVRANGE), three meanings.
- [R4 · The road ahead](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/the-road-ahead): the arc R4.01→R4.06 and the doors — into the EchoMQ course's Queue pillar (/echomq/queue, the schedule/promote/retry/lifecycle machinery) and the durable persistence floor (/echo-persistence) where a trimmed or archived job lands (EchoStore.Graft → Tigris).

## The granular modules (all built — each a hub + three dives; the workshop is a single-page capstone)

- [R4.01 · The delayed queue](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/delayed-queue): score a job by its fire-time, sweep by score — EchoMQ.Jobs.enqueue_at/5 + enqueue_in/5 write onto emq:{queue}:schedule (score = the run-at ms: enqueue_in is now+delay off the server TIME, enqueue_at the caller's absolute ms), and EchoMQ.Jobs.promote/3 (the @promote due-range sweep: ZRANGEBYSCORE schedule -inf now, ZREM, ZADD pending 0). EchoMQ.Metronome is the next-wake (one BLPOP emq:{q}:wake beat). Dives: [score-is-fire-time](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/delayed-queue/score-is-fire-time) · [zrangebyscore-promotion](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/delayed-queue/zrangebyscore-promotion) · [the-next-wake](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/delayed-queue/the-next-wake). ✓ built
- [R4.02 · Schedulers & repeatable jobs](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/schedulers): recurring jobs on the EchoMQ.Repeat registry (emq:{q}:repeat ZSET by next-run + a per-name record hash), upserted via an EXISTS guard so a boot adds no duplicate; EchoMQ.Pump sweeps due entries and mints a fresh branded JOB id each tick; the real form is interval (every_ms), cron the classic naming. Dives: [cron-vs-interval](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/schedulers/cron-vs-interval) · [upsert-no-duplicates](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/schedulers/upsert-no-duplicates) · [start-to-start-cadence](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/schedulers/start-to-start-cadence). ✓ built
- [R4.03 · Priority with composite scores](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/priority-scores): the Redis pattern packs a priority tier and an arrival counter into one score (tier in the high bits, arrival in the low, read by ZPOPMIN). The EchoMQ application is the honest contrast: numeric per-job priority is retired by design (lanes.ex) — precedence is a property of the lane, not the job, served by per-lane fair-share weight (EchoMQ.Lanes.weight/4 + wclaim/3, the @gwclaim ring rotation), and arrival order is the branded JOB id's mint order on score-0 sets (the order theorem). Dives: [packing-two-keys-in-one-score](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/priority-scores/packing-two-keys-in-one-score) · [fifo-within-tier](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/priority-scores/fifo-within-tier) · [zpopmin](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/priority-scores/zpopmin). ✓ built
- [R4.04 · Backoff & retry](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/backoff-retry): a retry is a delayed job whose fire-time a backoff formula computes — EchoMQ.Backoff.delay_ms/2 owns the math ({:exponential, base, cap} → min(base × 2^(attempts-1), cap), plus full jitter), EchoMQ.Jobs.retry/7 reschedules onto the same emq:{q}:schedule set under the attempts fence, and promote/3 sweeps the retry back when due. Dives: [exponential-backoff](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/backoff-retry/exponential-backoff) · [jitter-thundering-herd](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/backoff-retry/jitter-thundering-herd) · [reuse-the-delayed-zset](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/backoff-retry/reuse-the-delayed-zset). ✓ built
- [R4.05 · Leaderboards](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/leaderboards): the sorted set as a ranking — ZADD a score, ZREVRANK/ZREVRANGE read rank, top-N, and around-me; the rank is computed on read, never stored. Grounded in the codemojex board (Codemojex.Board.record/3 best-of ZADD onto cm:<game>:board, Codemojex.Board.top/2 ZREVRANGE … WITHSCORES, score = Codemojex.Scoring.points). Dives: [zadd-and-zrank](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/leaderboards/zadd-and-zrank) · [top-n-and-around-me](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/leaderboards/top-n-and-around-me) · [the-score-update-path](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/leaderboards/the-score-update-path). ✓ built
- [R4.06 · Workshop](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority/workshop) — the capstone, one page, no dives: EchoMQ schedules codemojex's notification and digest jobs (Codemojex.NotificationWorker over the cm.notify queue), folding all five modules — a lesson-style reminder on the schedule set, a recurring digest on EchoMQ.Repeat, a backed-off resend via EchoMQ.Backoff onto the schedule set, fair delivery across player lanes, and the round leaderboard ranking. ✓ built

## The doors

R4 opens onto the EchoMQ course — the Queue pillar (/echomq/queue): the schedule/promote path, the retry/backoff machinery, lifecycle controls, and lanes/groups, the bus internals rung by rung. And onto the durable persistence floor (/echo-persistence): where a trimmed or archived job lands (EchoStore.StreamArchive → EchoStore.Graft → Tigris), the narrative of bcs.5.md.

## Up

- [Course home](/redis-patterns) · [R3 · Reliable Queues](/redis-patterns/queues) · next: [R5 · Streams & Events](/redis-patterns/streams-events) (soon)
